mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, offering significantly higher throughput and lower resource usage. It is ideal for applications requiring high-performance caching and data storage without code changes. Key trade-offs include its reliance on Kubernetes for deployment and the need for careful resource management.

steps.title: Add Dragonfly Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup, allowing you to install Dragonfly easily.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflydb.github.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Dragonfly Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dragonfly to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

```

> This command installs the Dragonfly CRDs.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dragonfly application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the version and namespace.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Installs Dragonfly in the 'dragonfly' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Dragonfly Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Dragonfly custom resource to configure the Dragonfly instance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This command creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly is Running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dragonfly deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly

```

> This command lists the pods in the Dragonfly namespace.
> This command checks the status of the Dragonfly custom resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dragonfly installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated data and resources.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> This command uninstalls the Dragonfly Helm release.
> This command deletes the Dragonfly namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step will upgrade Dragonfly to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Backup the current Dragonfly configuration.
> This command upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dragonfly instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly custom resource may not have been created or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the Dragonfly custom resource definition and ensure it is applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly pods may be failing due to resource constraints or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dragonfly' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Dragonfly'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and check the service type and ports.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the Dragonfly pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your Kubernetes cluster or adjust the resource requests/limits in the Dragonfly configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new Redis commands and improved memory management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
